concerns.text: Task T014 implements gap filling with 'Fixed baseline (1360.5 W/m²)' for gaps ≥ 1 year. Spec FR-002 requires 'linear interpolation for gaps < 1 year' AND 'low-activity proxy' for gaps ≥ 1 year. The task description omits the mandatory linear interpolation step for short gaps, silently weakening the preprocessing requirement.

Task T015 specifies 'Time-Block Cross-Validation (Train: 2003-2015, Test: 2016-Present)'. Spec US-1 and SC-001 mandate 'leave-one-cycle-out' validation to account for cycle-to-cycle variability. Time-Block CV on a fixed date range does not satisfy the 'leave-one-cycle-out' constraint, weakening the validation strategy.

Task T014 implements 'Feature engineering: 'Cycle Phase' (sin/cos of day-of-year)'. Spec FR-003 and Constitution Principle VI explicitly mandate 'utilizing Cycle ID (from SILSO definitions) as a categorical feature'. The task replaces the required categorical feature with a phase proxy, violating the core modeling constraint.

Task T020 describes applying the 'trained RF/GP model' and a 'Cycle-Agnostic fallback'. Spec FR-003 and FR-004 require a distinct 'Cycle-Agnostic fallback model (GSN-only)' to be trained and used for unseen cycles. The task implies a fallback logic within the main model rather than a separate GSN-only model architecture, weakening the fallback requirement.

Task T029 sweeps 'inconsistency tolerance threshold' over absolute differences. Spec FR-009 defines this threshold as 'maximum allowed absolute difference between cycle-specific calibration coefficients'. Since the tasks (T014/T015) removed the 'cycle-specific calibration coefficients' (by removing Cycle ID features), the metric defined in FR-009 no longer exists in the implementation, making the task impossible to execute as specified.
